basics.name: Amit Rana
basics.title: Senior Software Development Engineer (SDE-2)
basics.headline: Senior SDE @ Truminds | Building reliable AI-assisted & agentic developer workflows | MS Informatics

about.summaryShort: Full-stack developer & problem solver. React/Next + Node, plus developer tooling and AI-assisted workflows. Maintainer of Hookpilot.
about.summaryProfessional: Full-stack developer who cares more about solving the actual problem than about which layer it lives in. Work runs from React and Next.js on the frontend to Node on the backend, with a recent focus on developer tooling and AI-assisted / agentic development workflows.
currentFocus: Maintaining Hookpilot — 50k+ weekly downloads on npm
Building Ghostframes — zero-config skeleton loaders for React
Tooling around AI-assisted and agentic development workflows
values: Reliability and maintainability over throwaway code
Verifying what AI agents produce before relying on it
Technology-agnostic by principle — pick the right tool for the problem
Performance, accessibility, and SEO as first-class concerns
File-only, local-first tooling with no hidden backends or telemetry

roles.title: Senior Software Development Engineer (SDE-2)
highlights: Led full-stack delivery for production apps, from React/Next.js/TypeScript frontends to the services behind them, prioritizing reliability and maintainability over throwaway code.
Brought AI-assisted and agentic workflows into the team's process, with a focus on verifying what agents produce before relying on it.
Hit 90+ Lighthouse performance scores, with FCP under 1.2s, LCP under 2s, and TTFB under 300ms.
Shipped WCAG-compliant interfaces (90+ Lighthouse accessibility) using semantic HTML, ARIA roles, and full keyboard navigation.
Cut application load time by 40% through targeted performance work.
Architected and shipped a scalable frontend used by 300K+ users with Next.js and Tailwind CSS, achieving 95+ Core Web Vitals across performance, SEO, and accessibility.
Integrated PWA features to boost mobile engagement and user retention by 30%.
Implemented Zustand for global state management and i18n for multilingual support.
Developed REST API integration workflows with a .NET-based backend, improving performance and maintainability.
Mentored junior engineers on writing clean, well-tested code.

roles.title: Software Development Engineer (SDE-1)
highlights: Developed interactive and responsive UIs using React.js, JavaScript, and HTML/CSS to enhance user engagement.
Contributed to a major client delivery, improving platform responsiveness and reducing bugs by 30%.
Received early recognition for quality contributions, leading to a promotion to Senior Engineer.

roles.title: Teaching Assistant
highlights: Debugged student code and helped with assignments and projects.

certifications.name: Data Structures in JAVA Excellence Certificate
MySQL Database Administration: Beginner SQL Database Design

projects.name: Hookpilot
projects.description: A flexible Git hook manager that lets developers automate workflows and enforce coding standards across the SDLC. Ships a library of ready-to-use hooks (linting, formatting, testing, security checks, commit-message validation, branch protection, large-file checks) plus an interactive CLI for adding, restoring, listing, and removing hooks, with support for fully custom shell/Node scripts.
features: Predefined hooks for common pre-commit / commit-msg / pre-push / post-merge / post-checkout tasks
Interactive CLI: init, add, restore, remove, list
Per-project configuration via hooks-config.json
Custom script support with exit-code conventions
--no-verify override support

projects.name: Coherence
projects.tagline: Documentation-drift detector & repair plugin for Claude Code
projects.description: A Claude Code plugin that detects documentation drift (code changing without its docs) and proposes surgical patches via a two-stage LLM pipeline. Runs as Claude Code hooks + slash commands; team-distributable surfaces live as committed files with no backend ever. Includes a trust-ladder auto-apply gate, an asserts: frontmatter validation system with eight engines, a corpus audit, and cosign-signed releases.
features: Two-stage LLM pipeline: Stage 1 canonical selection, Stage 2 patch writer with deterministic validation (format → apply → change-class recount → line ratio → prompt-injection → hallucination grep → asserts)
Graduated mode with per-section trust gate (score ≥ 0.85); never auto-applies destructive or frontmatter changes
asserts: frontmatter contracts with 8 validation engines (text-pattern + codebase-linked)
Per-developer trust ledger with 30-day half-life decay scoring
Corpus audit with token-budget classifier + optional LLM consistency pass
Sigstore cosign keyless OIDC signed releases, verifiable via Rekor
architecturalCommitments: No backend, ever (file-only; git is the substrate)
No legacy version support — each major stands alone

projects.name: Ghostframes
projects.description: Zero-config skeleton loaders for React, shipped as the single public package @ghostframes/runtime. Provides build-time capture that generates skeleton manifests from real components, plus CI quality gates (validate / diff / report) that enforce schema, key coverage, invalid-entry budget, and artifact size.
features: AutoSkeleton component + generateStaticBlueprint helper
Build-time manifest capture with generated loader artifacts
CI quality gates: ghostframes validate / diff / report
Layered build → CI → deploy workflow

projects.name: GitCompass
projects.description: A local-first Git analytics tool that reads commit history and surfaces hotspots (high churn, too many authors), velocity spikes, contributor burnout signals, bus-factor risk, and onboarding guidance. Built as a modular monorepo and shipped as two packages: git-compass (CLI) and @git-compass/web (visual dashboard with heatmaps, churn charts, and a plain-English AI query interface). Everything runs locally — no account, cloud, or telemetry.
features: File hotspot detection (churn + author concentration)
Code-velocity spike detection
Contributor burnout / after-hours pattern signals
Bus-factor risk (single-owner files)
Plain-English AI querying over the repo; multi-SDK support
100% local — no account, cloud, or telemetry

projects.name: Glassbox
projects.tagline: Local, read-only Claude Code session observer
projects.description: A local, read-only Claude Code session observer. Reads a Claude Code JSONL transcript and renders a single self-contained HTML report with six behavioural metrics, a session timeline, and a harness-health scorecard. Makes no network calls and never controls the agent. Zero-dependency, plain ESM, no build step — installable straight from GitHub. Built intentionally meta, under its own minimal harness, to illustrate what it measures.
features: Single self-contained HTML report with session timeline + six-metric scorecard
Auto-discovery of the latest transcript across known Claude roots (--latest)
Two-session comparison mode
Secret redaction (--redact), scope globs, configurable loop threshold
Zero network calls; never controls the agent

projects.name: mcp-sentry
projects.tagline: Security linter for TypeScript MCP servers
projects.description: A static-analysis security linter for TypeScript Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Detects all eight OWASP MCP Top 10 categories (MCP01–MCP08), grades the project A–F, and integrates with CI via SARIF, PR comments, and a public Shields.io badge. Includes inter-procedural taint tracking for command injection and intent-subversion detection for read-only-named tools that mutate state.
features: All eight OWASP MCP Top 10 checks (MCP01–MCP08)
A–F project grade with --fail-on threshold for CI
Reporters: text / JSON / SARIF / Markdown
Inter-procedural taint tracking (command injection)
Cloudflare Durable Object rate limiter + optional HMAC-SHA256 request signing
GitHub Action + public badge endpoint

projects.name: JavaScript-Verse
projects.tagline: In-browser JavaScript & React playground with live execution
projects.description: A browser-based code execution environment and markdown editor for writing and running JavaScript and React. Supports runtime NPM modules, React component creation and rendering, API calls, side-by-side docs, a coding cheatsheet, and code export. Solves safe code execution via iframe isolation, advanced JS/JSX via in-browser Babel transpilation, and import handling via in-browser bundling.
features: In-browser JavaScript & React execution with live preview
Runtime support for any NPM module
Iframe-isolated, sandboxed code execution
Babel-based in-browser transpilation of JSX/advanced syntax
In-browser bundling of imports
Side-by-side docs, cheatsheet, and code export

projects.name: FileSqueeze
projects.description: A lightweight command-line tool that compresses and decompresses text-based files (.txt, .json, .docx, .pdf) using a custom Huffman coding implementation. Generates binary output with stored Huffman-tree metadata for decompression and reports compression ratio and original/compressed sizes.
features: Custom Huffman coding compression / decompression
Supports .txt, .json, .docx, .pdf (text portion)
Binary output with serialized tree metadata
Per-file compression report (ratio + sizes)

projects.name: SkinBattle.gg
projects.description: Real-time CS:GO skin battle platform with live multiplayer interactions over WebSockets.

projects.name: Felis AI
projects.description: A private research and memory chatbot combining retrieval, persistent memory, and conversational AI.

projects.name: JSON Parser
projects.description: A Node.js JSON parser and validator that checks structural correctness (mismatched/extra brackets, trailing commas, invalid control characters) and parses valid JSON into JavaScript objects, with detailed error messages. Supports floating-point, scientific notation, and all standard JSON types.
